  1. 🇮🇹 Days 7-9: Naples (Jun 11-13)
  2. Naples Overview

Naples Overview

Archaeology, Pizza & Southern Italian Soul

naples
regions
planning
Published

June 10, 2026

About Naples

Napoli - Raw, chaotic, deeply cultured, and home to some of the world’s finest archaeology and the best pizza on Earth. Southern Italy’s largest city is gritty, loud, and utterly authentic - a place that doesn’t try to please tourists but rewards those who embrace its energy.

Our Time Here: 2 full days (June 11-12) + arrival afternoon (June 10)

Why Naples? - Gateway to Herculaneum (better than Pompeii) - World-class archaeological museum (MANN) - Birthplace of pizza - Authentic southern Italian culture - Dramatic setting on the Bay of Naples with Vesuvius backdrop

Trip Context

Arrival (June 10)

High-speed train from Florence (~2.5 hours), arriving early afternoon. Check into hotel, orientation walk, first taste of Neapolitan pizza.

Full Days (June 11-12)

Day 1 (June 11): Herculaneum guided tour (morning/midday), MANN museum or Cappella Sansevero, evening in historic center

Day 2 (June 12): MANN museum (if not done yesterday), Spaccanapoli district, Cappella Sansevero, waterfront walk, final Naples evening

Departure (June 13)

Morning checkout, train to Puglia (Brindisi) - scenic 5-6 hour journey through southern Italy.

Naples at a Glance

The Authentic South

Naples is everything Florence isn’t: chaotic instead of refined, gritty instead of polished, loud instead of reserved. But it’s deeply rewarding:

  • History: Greek colony (8th century BC), major Roman city, capital of Kingdom of Naples
  • Culture: Opera (invented here), commedia dell’arte, Neapolitan song
  • Archaeology: Guarding the treasures of Pompeii and Herculaneum
  • Food: Pizza Margherita was invented here (1889)
  • Character: Napoletani are passionate, expressive, and proud

Fair Warning: Naples has rough edges. It’s not dangerous, but it requires awareness and an open mind. The rewards - incredible food, world-class art, genuine Italian life - far outweigh any initial culture shock.

Major Attractions

Archaeological Sites (Our Priority)

Herculaneum ⭐ TOP PRIORITY - Better preserved than Pompeii (sealed by volcanic mud, not ash) - Upper floors, wooden furniture, intact frescoes - The Boat Houses: 300 skeletons visible, most powerful archaeological moment in Italy - 20 minutes by Circumvesuviana train from Naples - Book guided tour in advance (essential for understanding)

Mount Vesuvius (Optional) - The volcano that destroyed Pompeii and Herculaneum (AD 79) - Hike to crater rim (~30 min uphill) - Incredible Bay of Naples views - Can combine with Herculaneum tour - Still active (last eruption 1944)

Museums

MANN - Museo Archeologico Nazionale ⭐ ESSENTIAL - World’s finest collection of Roman artifacts - Most treasures from Pompeii & Herculaneum - Alexander Mosaic (from House of the Faun, Pompeii) - Farnese Collection (massive Roman sculptures) - Secret Cabinet (Roman erotic art) - Egyptian collection - Visit AFTER Herculaneum for context

Cappella Sansevero ⭐ MUST SEE - Giuseppe Sanmartino’s Veiled Christ (1753) - Marble veil appears translucent (one of world’s most astonishing sculptures) - Tiny chapel - book timed entry ahead - 30-45 minutes, unforgettable - No photography allowed

Churches & Historic Sites

Duomo di Napoli (Cathedral) - Chapel of San Gennaro (Naples’ patron saint) - His blood “liquefies” 3 times a year (miraculous) - Baroque architecture

Castel Nuovo (Maschio Angioino) - Medieval castle - Civic Museum - Views of harbor

Napoli Sotterranea (Underground Naples) - 40 meters below street level - Greek aqueducts, Roman theater, WWII shelters - Guided tours only

Neighborhoods

Centro Storico (Historic Center)

Spaccanapoli - Arrow-straight street splitting old Naples - Follows ancient Greek city plan (2,400 years old!) - Churches, shops, street life - Via dei Tribunali parallel street (pizza central)

What to See: - Chiesa del Gesù Nuovo (Baroque church) - Santa Chiara (monastery, beautiful cloister) - San Domenico Maggiore (Gothic church) - Street vendors, nativity workshops, local life

Character: Chaotic, loud, hanging laundry, narrow streets, Vespas everywhere - authentic Naples.

Quartieri Spagnoli (Spanish Quarters)

  • Grid of narrow streets west of Spaccanapoli
  • Working-class neighborhood
  • Maradona murals (he’s a god here)
  • Excellent cheap food
  • Not touristy

Safety Note: Fine during day, avoid at night.

Chiaia & Vomero

Chiaia: - Upscale shopping district - Via Chiaia (shopping street) - Safer, cleaner than centro storico - Good hotels and restaurants

Vomero: - Hilltop neighborhood - Residential, quieter - Castel Sant’Elmo (fortress with city views) - Certosa di San Martino (monastery museum) - Funicular from below

Lungomare (Waterfront)

  • Via Partenope and Via Caracciolo
  • Promenade along Bay of Naples
  • Vesuvius views
  • Castel dell’Ovo (seaside castle)
  • Excellent seafood restaurants
  • Cleaner, prettier than centro

Recommended for: Sunset walks, jogging, escaping chaos

Getting Around

Metro

Two Lines: - Line 1 (Orange): Connects Vomero, Centro Storico, station - Line 2 (Blue): Connects Napoli Centrale to Pozzuoli

Tickets: €1.10 for 100 minutes Art Stations: Some stations are museums (Toledo, Università stunning)

Circumvesuviana

Commuter Rail: - Lower level of Napoli Centrale (Garibaldi) station - Goes to: Herculaneum (Ercolano Scavi), Pompeii, Sorrento - €2.80 to Herculaneum - Old trains, watch belongings (pickpockets)

Funicular

Three funiculars to Vomero: - Montesanto, Centrale, Chiaia - €1.10 tickets (same as metro) - Scenic ride up hill

Walking

Centro Storico walkable but: - Chaotic traffic (cars, Vespas, pedestrians all mixed) - Narrow sidewalks or none - Watch for Vespas on sidewalks (yes, really) - Not as pedestrian-friendly as Florence

Pro Tip: Cross streets with confidence (hesitation is dangerous).

Taxis

  • White cars with “TAXI” sign and meter
  • Use official taxis only (avoid touts)
  • Set fare from airport (~€20-25 to center)
  • Apps: Free Now, it Taxi

Scam Alert: Always insist on meter or agree on price before starting.

Food & Wine

Pizza (The Whole Reason to Visit)

Naples invented pizza. This is where it’s best.

Classic Napoletana: - Thin, soft, charred crust - San Marzano tomatoes - Buffalo mozzarella - Basil - Wood-fired oven (900°F, 90 seconds)

Must-Try Pizzerias:

Da Michele (Since 1870) - Only 2 pizzas: Marinara or Margherita - €5-7, cash only - Expect lines - Pure, simple, perfect

Sorbillo (Via dei Tribunali) - More variety - Always packed - Reservations possible for dinner

50 Kalò (Modern, upscale) - Gourmet pizza - Higher prices (€12-18) - Reservation essential

Rule: If you only eat one meal in Naples, make it pizza.

Other Neapolitan Specialties

Pasta: - Spaghetti alle vongole (with clams) - Pasta alla Genovese (onion ragù, no basil despite name)

Seafood: - Fritto misto (mixed fried seafood) - Polpo (octopus) - Fresh fish (all from Bay of Naples or nearby waters)

Street Food: - Sfogliatella - Shell-shaped pastry (ricotta filling) - Baba al rhum - Rum-soaked sponge cake - Pizza fritta - Fried pizza (filled, folded, deep-fried) - Cuoppo - Fried seafood in paper cone

Coffee: - Espresso culture serious here - €1 at bar (standing) - Never cappuccino after 11 AM

Where to Eat

See: Restaurants page for specific recommendations.

General Tips: - Eat pizza on Via dei Tribunali - Seafood on Lungomare - Cheap local food in Quartieri Spagnoli - Skip tourist traps near port

Practical Information

When We’re There

June 10-12: Early summer - Weather: High 80s-90°F (30-32°C), humid - Crowds: Moderate (fewer tourists than Florence) - Sunset: ~8:30 PM

Safety

Naples has a reputation but it’s largely outdated:

Actual Risks: - Pickpockets: Watch bags in crowded areas, on metro, Circumvesuviana - Bag snatching: Keep purses zipped, crossbody style - Scams: Restaurant touts, overpriced taxis, fake tour guides

NOT a Risk: - Violent crime against tourists (extremely rare) - Wandering during day (perfectly safe)

Be Smart: - Don’t flash expensive jewelry, cameras - Keep valuables close in crowds - Use hotel safe for passports - Stay aware of surroundings - Trust instincts

Neighborhoods to Avoid at Night: - Quartieri Spagnoli (okay during day) - Immediately around Napoli Centrale station - Industrial areas

Safe at Night: - Chiaia - Vomero - Lungomare - Parts of centro storico (stick to main streets)

Money

Budget Per Day (Per Person): - Lodging: €60-100 (shared room) - Food: €25-40 (pizza is cheap!) - Museums/tours: €20-80 (Herculaneum tour is biggest expense) - Transport: €5-10 - Total: €110-230 per day

Cheaper than Florence!

Useful Phrases

Neapolitan Dialect different from standard Italian:

  • Buongiorno → “Buon’ jorn”
  • Grazie → “Grazzie” (strong Z)
  • Come sta? → “Comm’ sta?”

They’ll appreciate any attempt at Italian (or Neapolitan).

Tips for Naples

Embrace the Chaos

Naples is overwhelming at first: - Traffic is insane - Streets are loud - Everything feels disorganized

But that’s the point. Naples doesn’t perform for tourists. It’s authentically itself - take it or leave it. Once you stop expecting Florence’s refinement, you’ll love Naples’ raw energy.

Don’t Compare to Florence

Different cities, different vibes: - Florence: Museum city, refined, Renaissance - Naples: Living city, gritty, authentic

Both are incredible. Judge Naples on its own terms.

The Herculaneum Decision

Most tourists go to Pompeii. We’re going to Herculaneum because: - Better preserved (organic materials intact) - Smaller, more manageable (not overwhelming) - Fewer crowds - More emotionally powerful (the Boat Houses) - Closer to Naples (20 min vs. 40 min)

If you have time for only one: Herculaneum > Pompeii.

MANN Museum Timing

Go AFTER Herculaneum: - Seeing the ruins first gives context - Artifacts make more sense - You’ll appreciate what you’re looking at

Not before - it’s backwards chronologically.

Our Naples Itinerary at a Glance

Day 1 (June 11): - Morning/midday: Herculaneum guided tour - Afternoon: MANN museum OR Cappella Sansevero (depending on energy) - Evening: Pizza on Via dei Tribunali, Spaccanapoli walk

Day 2 (June 12): - Morning: MANN museum (if not done) OR Cappella Sansevero - Midday: Spaccanapoli district exploration - Afternoon: Lungomare waterfront walk, Castel dell’Ovo - Evening: Farewell dinner (seafood or pizza), gelato, packing for Puglia

See Daily Itineraries in sidebar for detailed schedules.

Useful Links

  • Naples Tourism
  • MANN Museum
  • Parco Archeologico di Ercolano (Herculaneum)

Transportation

  • ANM (Naples public transport)
  • Circumvesuviana

Useful

  • Naples Metro Art Stations
  • Naples Underground
